design_philosophy.core_loop: Select race/season mode → customize car setup (spoilers, tyres, gearbox) → compete against 11 real-life F1 drivers → mandatory pit stop for tyre change based on weather conditions → finish race and progress through season
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time racing execution against licensed AI drivers with dynamic weather affecting tyre strategy and grip
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Assume the role of Nigel Mansell and relive his 1992 F1 Championship-winning season, competing authentically against period-accurate rivals
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Arcade-style racing with mandatory pit strategy (tyre changes) rather than pure simulation; emphasis on accessible F1 fantasy through licensing and character customization (nationality selection) while maintaining Mansell's iconic visual identity (mustache, cap, blue suit). Treats rival drivers as individuals rather than constructor pairs, differentiating from standard F1 format.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can adjust car setup pre-race (spoiler, gearbox, tyre selection) and must dynamically respond to weather changes via tyre strategy during mandatory pit stops. Skill modes available: single race, season competition, or practice on specific tracks.

progression.upgrade_system: Pre-race car customization system allowing adjustment of spoilers, tyres (hard/soft/rain based on weather), and gearbox settings
progression.unlock_mechanism: Season mode progression across multiple races; player advances by competing against 11 real-life drivers from 1992 F1 season
replayability_hooks: Practice mode for skill development on individual tracks
Custom character creation allowing player to select from 17+ countries while retaining Mansell's visual appearance
Dynamic weather affecting tyre strategy and race outcomes

world_building_techniques: Licensed 1992 F1 season setting with historically accurate drivers and team colours
National anthems played on podium based on winning driver's nationality
Character customization preserving Mansell's iconic visual identity (signature mustache and cap in blue suit)
Authentic race track selection from 1992 season

faction_indicators: 11 competing F1 drivers from 1992 season, each with distinct team affiliation (constructor colours respected)
Player nationality selection creating implicit allegiance during podium ceremonies
danger_signals: Dynamic weather transitions requiring tactical tyre swaps during mandatory pit stops
Real-life competitive drivers with period-accurate difficulty scaling
environmental_storytelling: Track design reflecting actual 1992 F1 circuits
Team colours visually distinguishing rival vehicles (only Mansell shown in blue suit; all others in yellow racing suits)
Podium ceremony with corresponding national anthem reinforcing geographic/national identity
